---
title: "Lecture_Testing"
author: "Emma Rasmussen"
format: html
editor: visual
---

## Lecture Testing May 26, 2026

Quarto enables you to weave together content and executable code into a finished document. To learn more about Quarto see <https://quarto.org>.

## Install Package

When you click the **Render** button a document will be generated that includes both content and the output of embedded code. You can embed code like this:

```{r}
#install.packages("testthat")
library(testthat)
library(tidyverse)
library(devtools)
```

Testthat package

Functions expect_XXXXX(input) examples:

expect_true(condition)

expect_equal(value, value)

```{r}

# matches 
fish1= "salmon"
fish2= "trout"
expect_match(fish1,fish2) # not a match error message 

# length
a = seq(from=1, to=10)
expect_length(a, 100) # gives error & actual length 

```

Practice with NPV

```{r}
library(here)


compute_NPV = function(value, time, discount) {
  result = value / (1 + discount)**time
  return(result)
}
compute_NPV

#source(here("esm-262/compute_NPV.R"))
futurevalue = 0
expect_equal(compute_NPV(value=futurevalue, time=10, discount=0.01),0)
futurevalue = 100
expect_true(compute_NPV(value=futurevalue, time=10, discount=0.01) > 0)
futurevalue = -100
expect_true(compute_NPV(value=futurevalue, time=10, discount=0.01) < 0)

```

```{r}

# lets introduce a bug in the function to see how tests catch it
compute_NPV = function(value, time, discount) {
  # use value to compute NPV
  result = value - (1 + discount)**time
  return(result)
}
expect_equal(compute_NPV(value=0, time=10, discount=0.01),0)
```

Could write a test with input value

Example using the summary function

```{r}
# generate some test data
testdata = rnorm(mean=25, sd=3, n=100)
summary(testdata)

# it is actually returning a named vector — what are the names?
names(summary(testdata))

# now we can access specific elements
summary(testdata)["Max."]

# and use in a test to make sure this function works
# the max should be greater than the min, right?

expect_true((summary(testdata)["Max."] - summary(testdata)["Min."]) > 0)



```

Quick Check Quiz

```{r}
# 
summary(testdata)["Mean"]
expect_true(summary(testdata)["Mean"] > 24 & summary(testdata)["Mean"] < 26)

```

```{r}

effective_rain = function(rain, interceptC=1){
  totalrain = sum(rain)
  raineff = rain - interceptC
  totalraineff = sum(raineff)
  return(list(totalrain = totalrain, totalraineff= totalraineff))
}
# simple tests
# if it never rains for 730 days, both totalrain and totalraineff should be zero
raindata = rep(0, times= 730)
expect_equal(effective_rain(raindata)$totalrain, 0)
# so far so good, now for effective rainfall
# should subtract intercept on days when 0, flaw in thinking not code 

effective_rain=function(rain, interceptC=1) {
    
  # easy part - get total rain
  totalrain = sum(rain)
  
  # interception happens every day so we need to apply it per day
  # pmax ensures effective rain can't go below zero
  raineff = rain-interceptC
  raineff = pmax(raineff,0)
  totalraineff = sum(raineff)
  
  return(list(totalrain=totalrain, totalraineff=totalraineff))}

raindata = rep(0, times=730)
expect_that(effective_rain(raindata)$totalraineff, equals(0)) # same as below 
#expect_equal(effective_rain(raindata)$totalrain, 0)

```

NPV testing Lecture Thursday 5/28/2026

```{r}
source(here("lecture/tests/compute_NPV.R"))
# if profit is 0, NPV should be 0
# if time is 0, NPV should equal the original value

# now lets write the test that combines several expectations
test_that("compute_NPV_works", {

expect_equal(compute_NPV(value=0,time=100,discount=0.1), 0)
expect_equal(compute_NPV(value=100, time=0, discount=0.1), 100)
})

# now lets add a test that checks that NPV is higher for a shorter time period
test_that("compute_NPV_time_works", {
testdata = c(2, 4)
short = compute_NPV(100, time=testdata[1], 0.01)
long = compute_NPV(100, time = testdata[2], 0.01)
expect_true(short > long)
})

```

Using Test Directory

```{r}
# source original function
source(here("esm262/lecture/tests/compute_NPV.R"))
# run tests in a file
test_file(here("esm262/lecture/tests/test_compute_NPV.R"))

# now try  version with error checking
source(here("course-materials/R/compute_NPV_witherror.R"))
test_file(here("course-materials/tests/test_compute_NPV.R"))
```

Test Example: Spring Temperatures from climate data

Input: year/month/ average temp (tavg)

optional parameters: spring

```{r}
# source function
source(here("lecture/tests/spring_summary.R"))
spring_summary
```

Create test data

```{r}
# to test this function we need to create some data, we can
# add days and precip even though function doesn't use them to make sure it doesn't
# mess up the function
clim_data = as.data.frame(cbind(month=c(1:4), day=rep(1, times=4), year=c(2000,2000,2001,2001), 
                                 precip=rep(0, times=4), tavg=c(2,2,2,4)))
spring_summary(clim_data)
```

Run Function

```{r}
spring_summary(clim_data, spring_months=c(1,4))
```

Test Function

```{r}
# we can embed data directly in a test
# save this to a file called test_spring_summary.R
test_that("spring_summary_works" ,
{clim_data = as.data.frame(cbind(month=c(1:4), day=rep(1, times=4), year=c(2000,2000,2001,2001), 
                                 precip=rep(0, times=4), tavg=c(2,2,2,4)))
expect_equal(spring_summary(clim_data, spring_months=4)$Tavg_spring, 4)
expect_equal(spring_summary(clim_data, spring_months=2)$Tavg_spring, 2)
expect_equal(spring_summary(clim_data, spring_months=c(1:4))$warmest_spring, 2001)

})
```

Test if one of the columns in missing

```{r}

expect_message(spring_summary(clim_data = c(7))) #if it finds an error it is true not message


```
